decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to control the game and lock down opponent's plays. With cards like Ugin, Karn, and The One Ring, it aims to establish a strong board presence and disrupt opponent's strategy. By generating mana with Urza's lands and Grim Monolith, the deck can power out big threats like Emrakul or Cityscape Leveler. Trinisphere and Planar Nexus slow down the opponent, while cards like Manifold Key and Voltaic Key provide additional utility. Overall, the deck aims to control the game through artifact prison effects and overwhelming threats.

This deck focuses on utilizing Cloudpost and Tron lands to quickly ramp up mana production in order to cast powerful spells and abilities. By combining Ugin, Karn, and other artifact cards, the deck aims to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The inclusion of cards like Planar Nexus and The One Ring provide additional win conditions and support for the deck's ramp and control strategy. Overall, this deck aims to establish dominance through its ramping capabilities and powerful artifact synergies.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with creatures such as Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis, and to use cards like Stitcher's Supplier and Elvish Reclaimer to help enable this strategy. Additionally, the deck relies on sacrificing creatures to power up Wight of the Reliquary and to fuel Hogaak's ability. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Thoughseize and Fatal Push to control the opponent's board. The land base is focused on mana fixing and graveyard interaction, with cards like Bojuka Bog and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on quickly and efficiently cheating out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. It also includes ways to protect these combos with counterspells like Flusterstorm and Force of Will. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with powerful creatures and spells early in the game, while also having the flexibility to adapt to different situations with cards like Brainstorm and Ponder. The mana base is designed to support the combo pieces and ensure a smooth curve for casting key spells.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly assemble a combo using all spells without any lands in play. The deck relies on using fast mana sources such as Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide, along with rituals like Cabal Ritual and Dark Ritual, to cast spells like Balustrade Spy and Dread Return to quickly win the game or heavily disrupt the opponent. The deck also includes recursion cards like Memory's Journey and Agadeem's Awakening to keep the combo going. The key is to sequence the spells efficiently to achieve a quick victory.

The deck focuses on locking down the opponent's resources using artifacts and control elements. It utilizes cards like Urza's Saga, Candelabra of Tawnos, and Karn, the Great Creator to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of cards like The One Ring and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn provide powerful win conditions. Cards like Trinisphere and Kozilek's Command help control the opponent's spells and resources. The deck aims to slow down the game and grind out victories by overwhelming the opponent with powerful artifacts and controlling elements.

The main concept of this deck is to maintain a high tempo while aggressively attacking the opponent by constantly disrupting their strategy and controlling the board with efficient spells and creatures. The deck utilizes a mix of efficient threats like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent along with disruptive spells like Daze, Force of Will, and Lightning Bolt to keep the opponent on the back foot. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Mishra's Bauble and Brainstorm to ensure consistent card advantage and selection, allowing the player to stay ahead in the game.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creature spells combined with disruptive control spells to maintain tempo and control of the game. Using cards like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Cori-Steel Cutter, along with spells like Lightning Bolt and Daze, the deck aims to pressure the opponent while also disrupting their game plan. The inclusion of cards like Mishra's Bauble and Brainstorm help the deck stay consistent and find the necessary pieces to win quickly and efficiently. Additionally, the use of Wasteland and Boomerang Basics can disrupt the opponent's mana base, further setting them back and allowing this UR Tempo deck to dominate the game.

The main focus of this deck is to maintain control of the game while applying pressure on the opponent through efficient removal spells and disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Daze. The key strategy is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while developing a board presence with aggressive creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Murktide Regent. The deck also aims to generate card advantage with cards like Brainstorm and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. Additionally, the deck includes land destruction effects like Wasteland to further disrupt the opponent's mana base and slow down their game plan.

This deck focuses on assembling the Doomsday combo as quickly and efficiently as possible, using cards such as Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Brainstorm to set up the combo. With cards like Jace, Wielder of Mysteries and Thassa's Oracle as win conditions, the deck aims to win the game in one explosive turn. Additionally, the deck includes disruption in the form of Force of Will, Daze, and Thoughtseize to protect the combo. The key strategy is to carefully plan out the Doomsday pile and execute it successfully to achieve victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing disruptive creatures like Skyclave Apparition and Stoneforge Mystic to control the opponent's board while applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd. The deck also includes cards like Karakas and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base, while Aether Vial helps cheat creatures into play at instant speed. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value through flickering creatures and disrupting the opponent's game plan.

This deck focuses on using land-based control elements to disrupt the opponent while building up a powerful land base of its own. It utilizes cards like Life from the Loam, Crop Rotation, and Wasteland to control the board and disrupt the opponent's plans. The deck also includes powerful lands like The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale, Dark Depths, and Urza's Saga to generate value and pressure the opponent. By leveraging lands for both control and win conditions, this deck aims to outmaneuver and outlast the opponent in the long game.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently reanimate powerful creatures from the graveyard, such as Archon of Cruelty and Atraxa, Grand Unified, using cards like Reanimate and Animate Dead. By utilizing cards like Brainstorm, Thoughtseize, and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, the deck aims to set up the graveyard and protect its combo pieces. Additionally, cards like Daze and Force of Will provide disruption and control elements, while cards like Bitter Triumph and Flow State help generate card advantage and maintain tempo. The deck also includes a small amount of land disruption with Wasteland and Undercity Sewers.

The main goal of this combo deck is to quickly get out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn by using spells like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. By using fast mana sources like Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb, the deck aims to cheat out big threats ahead of schedule and overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes counterspells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect its combos. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and win the game before the opponent can establish their own board presence.

The deck focuses on using artifacts to control the board and lock down opponents, utilizing cards like Urza's Workshop, Karn the Great Creator, and Trinisphere to limit their options. The goal is to establish a prison-like strategy with cards such as Urza's Saga and The One Ring to disrupt the opponent's game plan and eventually overwhelm them with powerful creatures like Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. This deck aims to effectively shut down the opponent's plays while setting up for a strong late-game finish with massive threats.

This deck aims to use the combination of artifacts and creature cards to generate powerful synergies and potentially win the game in a single turn. By utilizing cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch, Goblin Engineer, and Urza's Saga, the deck can assemble key pieces quickly and efficiently. With the ability to generate mana using Lotus Petal and Mox Opal, as well as control the board with cards like Legion Extruder and Pinnacle Emissary, the deck can quickly overwhelm opponents with powerful combos and threats.
